products.title: Snow Zebra
products.body_html:
Sulawesi Shrimp Care Guide

Sulawesi shrimp do best in warm, alkaline water with very stable parameters. They can breed in captivity, but they react badly to sudden changes and “new tank” conditions. These snow zebra are kept and bred in Neocaridina parameter

Basic Care

  • Tank size: 10+ gallons (more water volume helps stability)

  • Temperature: 76–82°F (keep it consistently warm)

  • pH: 7.2–8.2

  • GH/KH: Moderate hardness (common targets: GH ~5–8, KH ~3–6)

  • TDS: Common target range 150–250 ppm (many aim ~160–200)

  • Diet: Biofilm/algae + quality shrimp food; add blanched veggies; small protein meals occasionally

  • Behavior: Peaceful, often shy; spends time grazing surfaces

  • Tankmates: Best in a species-only tank; avoid fish if you want steady breeding

Key Notes

  • Use a mature, cycled tank with lots of hardscape/plants for biofilm.

  • Add strong aeration (warm water holds less oxygen).

  • Keep ammonia/nitrite at 0 and nitrates low; do small, consistent water changes.

  • Remineralized RO/DI with Sulawesi-specific minerals often works better than random tap water.

  • Do a slow drip acclimation; they stress easily from parameter swings.

products.title: Pink Boxer
products.body_html:
Sulawesi Shrimp Care Guide

Sulawesi shrimp do best in warm, alkaline water with very stable parameters. They can breed in captivity, but they react badly to sudden changes and “new tank” conditions. These pink boxer are kept and bred in Neocaridina parameter. 

Basic Care

  • Behavior: Peaceful, often shy; spends time grazing surfaces

Key Notes

  • Add strong aeration (warm water holds less oxygen).

  • Keep ammonia/nitrite at 0 and nitrates low; do small, consistent water changes.

  • Remineralized RO/DI with Sulawesi-specific minerals often works better than random tap water.

  • Do a slow drip acclimation; they stress easily from parameter swings.

products.title: Anubias Stardust
products.body_html:
• Light: Low to medium (too much light can trigger algae on leaves)

  • CO₂: Not required, but improves growth and overall health

  • Temperature: 72–82 °F (22–28 °C)

  • pH: 6.0–7.5

  • Growth rate: Slow to moderate (varies by species; nana stays slow)

products.title: Extreme Blue Bolt
products.body_html:
Caridina Care Guide

Caridina shrimp stay healthy in stable, soft-water setups with planted tanks and low bioload. They show better color and breed well under consistent conditions.

Basic Care

  • Tank size: 10+ gallons

  • Temperature: 68–74°F

  • pH: 5.5–6.8

  • GH/KH: Soft water (low KH)

  • Diet: Biofilm, specialized shrimp foods, blanched veggies

  • Behavior: Peaceful and active

  • Tankmates: Best kept in species-only tanks

Key Notes

  • Avoid parameter swings.

  • Use a sponge filter to protect juveniles.

  • Provide leaf litter and moss for steady biofilm growth.

products.title: Bloody Mary
products.body_html:
Neocaridina Care Guide

Neocaridina shrimp stay healthy when you keep stable, clean freshwater conditions. They adapt well and breed often in planted tanks.

Basic Care

  • Tank size: 5+ gallons

  • Temperature: 70–78°F

  • pH: 6.5–7.8

  • GH/KH: Moderate hardness

  • Diet: Algae, biofilm, shrimp pellets, blanched veggies

  • Behavior: Peaceful and active

  • Tankmates: Only peaceful nano fish

Key Notes

  • Avoid sudden parameter changes.

  • Keep a sponge filter to protect juveniles.

  • Provide plants and hiding spots for steady growth.

products.title: Weeping
products.body_html:
Each mat is approximately 3x3 inch. 

Care Requirements

  • Lighting: Low to medium (medium light enhances the “weeping” effect)

  • Temperature: 20–28 °C (68–82 °F)

  • pH: 5.5–7.5

  • Water hardness: Soft to medium

  • CO₂: Not required, but improves density and growth

  • Fertilizer: Optional; light dosing keeps it lush and green

products.title: Taiwan
products.body_html:
Each mat is approximately 3x3 inch 

Care Requirements

  • Lighting: Low to medium (brighter light = denser growth)

  • Temperature: ~18–28 °C (65–82 °F)

  • pH: 5.5–7.5

  • Water hardness: Soft to moderately hard

  • CO₂: Not required, but improves growth and compactness

  • Fertilizer: Optional; small amounts help keep it lush

products.title: Spiky
products.body_html:
Each mat is approximately 3x3 inch

 

Care & requirements

  • Light: Low to medium (medium gives the best “spiky” form)

  • CO₂: Not required, but improves growth and density

  • Temperature: ~20–28 °C (68–82 °F)

  • pH: ~5.5–7.5

  • Growth rate: Slow to moderate

products.title: Fissiden Fontanus
products.body_html:
Each mat is approximately 3x3 inch

 

Care requirements

  • Light: Low to medium (medium brings out best form)

  • CO₂: Not required, but strongly improves fullness

  • Temperature: 18–26 °C (65–79 °F)

  • pH: 5.5–7.5

  • Growth rate: Slow

products.title: Flame
products.body_html:
Each mat is approximately 3x3 inch

  • CO₂: Not required, but improves height and density

  • Temperature: 20–28 °C (68–82 °F)

products.title: WCO breeder food (high protein)
products.body_html:
50g per packet
High protein diet specifically designed for Caridina shrimp, Neocaridina shrimp and Sulawesi Shrimp

This feed provides high meat protein nutrients required for shrimp breeding, supports the growth of juvenile shrimp, and promotes egg-laying in broodstock.

Juveniles can easily forage on it. It's works best with WCO Shrimps Food recommend to feed 2-3 times per week.

Storage method: seal the bag opening and store it in a cool place.

products.title: WCO Shrimp Food (complete)
products.body_html:
50g per package
Balanced diets specifically designed for Caridina shrimp, Neocaridina shrimp and Sulawesi Shrimp

This feed supplies all the essential nutrients required for daily needs, supports the growth of juvenile shrimp, and promotes egg-laying in broodstock. Juveniles can easily forage on it.

Storage method: seal the bag opening and store it in a cool place.
